title: The RealReal (REAL) Reports Q1 Loss, Tops Revenue Estimates
text: The RealReal reported Q1 2026 results with an EPS loss of $0.01, beating the forecasted $0.05 loss. Revenue of $190 million exceeded estimates of $188 million. GMV grew 24% year-over-year to $606 million. Gross merchandise volume reached record levels, continuing the company's strong growth momentum. The company achieved significant margin expansion with adjusted EBITDA of $13.1 million (6.9% margin) versus $4.1 million (2.6% margin) in Q1 2025. Operating leverage of 730 basis points demonstrates the scalability of the business model with AI-driven automation improvements. Active buyer count reached 1.083 million (trailing twelve months), growing 10% year-over-year, indicating strong platform engagement. Average order value increased 15% to $646, reflecting increased demand for premium luxury items. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance, now projecting GMV growth of 14%-16%, revenue growth of 11%-13%, and adjusted EBITDA margin of 8.1% at midpoint. The company emphasized the success of supply-unlocking initiatives through retail store expansion and partner networks, combined with customer service improvements and AI-driven operational excellence initiatives.
